name: human-review-gate
description: Pauses workflow execution for human approval. Sends a webhook notification with full execution context to a configured URL, then blocks until an approval or rejection signal is received via the platform API. Supports configurable timeout with optional auto-approve behavior.

NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL.description: URL to POST a JSON notification when the gate is triggered. The payload includes execution_id, workflow context, reason, and a direct link for approval. Use this to notify Slack, PagerDuty, email, or a custom review dashboard.

TIMEOUT_MINUTES.description: Maximum minutes to wait for human approval before timing out (1-1440). If AUTO_APPROVE_IF_TIMEOUT is true, the gate approves on timeout; otherwise it rejects.

AUTO_APPROVE_IF_TIMEOUT.description: When true, the gate auto-approves if the timeout expires without a human decision. When false (default), timeout causes rejection. WARNING: enabling this means unreviewed items proceed automatically.

REASON.description: Human-readable explanation shown in the approval request and webhook notification. Supports {{field}} interpolation from upstream node output.

APPROVERS.description: Comma-separated list of approver emails or usernames. Included in the webhook notification payload for routing to the correct reviewer.

INCLUDE_INPUT_IN_NOTIFICATION.description: When true, the full upstream input data is included in the webhook notification payload. Set to false for sensitive data that should not leave the platform.

WEBHOOK_SECRET.description: Optional HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for the webhook notification. When set, an X-Talos-Signature header is added to the webhook POST for payload verification.

output_shape.approved: Boolean: true if approved, false if rejected or timed out without auto-approve
output_shape.decision_source: How the decision was made: 'human', 'timeout_auto_approve', or 'timeout_reject'
output_shape.reviewer: The identity of the reviewer who approved/rejected (null for timeout decisions)
output_shape.wait_seconds: How many seconds the gate waited before receiving a decision
output_shape.notification_sent: Boolean: whether the webhook notification was successfully delivered

setup_instructions.title: Actor capability ceiling -- required before adding this node
setup_instructions.detail: The workflow owning actor MUST have max_capability_world: 'governance-node'. Run: grant_capability_ceiling(actor_id: '<actor_id>', world: 'governance-node'). Without this, the workflow will fail at execution time with a capability ceiling error.

setup_instructions.title: Configure notification webhook
setup_instructions.detail: Set NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL to the endpoint that should receive approval requests. For Slack, use an Incoming Webhook URL. For custom dashboards, implement a POST handler that accepts JSON with {execution_id, reason, approvers, input_data, timestamp}.

setup_instructions.title: Set approval timeout behavior
setup_instructions.detail: Set TIMEOUT_MINUTES (default: 60) and AUTO_APPROVE_IF_TIMEOUT (default: false). For safety-critical workflows, keep auto-approve disabled so unreviewed items are rejected. For low-risk workflows, enable auto-approve to prevent indefinite blocking.

setup_instructions.title: Approve or reject via MCP tool
setup_instructions.detail: When execution pauses at this node, call list_pending_approvals to see waiting approvals, then submit_workflow_approval(execution_id: '<id>', approved: true|false, reason: '...') to unblock the execution.
